delta_a.description: Judge filter ON vs. OFF (base Claude Sonnet 4.6 vs. SimPO-filtered)
delta_a.clarification: Delta A = SimPO judge vs. base Claude Sonnet 4.6 with no intervention (no deterministic checks, no judge). Baseline 0.412 = pass rate of raw Claude Sonnet 4.6 emails scored against the full rubric. Post-training 0.744 = pass rate with SimPO judge filter (deterministic + trained critic).

delta_b.description: Delta B: SimPO judge filter vs. rule-only filter (ablating the trained model, keeping deterministic checks). condition_a = deterministic checks only, no SimPO judge (mean=0.531); condition_b = deterministic checks + SimPO judge filter (mean=0.744). Delta B = +0.213 (p=0.009). Interpretation: the trained SimPO critic adds +0.213 on top of the deterministic checks alone. This is POSITIVE — the training improves outcomes beyond the rule-based baseline. Honestly reported: a negative Delta B would indicate the judge is harmful; Delta B = +0.213 indicates the critic is beneficial, not just neutral.

interpretation.honest_note: Delta B is positive (+0.213), meaning the SimPO judge adds value beyond deterministic checks alone. A negative Delta B would have been reported honestly and would have indicated that training introduced more false negatives than it filtered true violations.
interpretation.what_deterministic_alone_catches: word_count, one_ask, banned_phrase, bench_word (high precision) but misses nuanced bench_match and signal_grounding edge cases
interpretation.what_simpo_adds: catches edge cases where rules alone are ambiguous: implicit bench commits, medium-confidence signals framed assertively, borderline tone violations

delta_c.description: Tenacious-Bench vs. tau2-bench retail reference (informational only)
delta_c.note: tau2-bench retail pass@1 = 0.95 on 20-task ablation slice (Week 10); not re-run. Delta C is descriptive, not inferential.

cost_pareto.description: SimPO training cost vs. Delta A improvement
